experiment: F-EXP11 baseline: cross-domain body-text integration measurement at N=901
hypothesis: lesson_quality_fixer.py cross-domain check will surface 395+ actionable flags and measure two-tier baseline: raw mention rate (~18%) and cites-based integration rate (~30-35%)

pre_registration.target_metric: cites-based body-text integration rate: cross-domain Cites: headers where L-NNN also appears in section body
pre_registration.expected_baseline: 0.1% (L-964 substantive discussion rate) OR ~30% if measuring weaker citation mention
pre_registration.success_criterion: F-EXP11 opened, baseline measured, structural mechanism (lesson_quality_fixer.py check) implemented
pre_registration.falsification: if structural flag has zero adoption rate at S449 without additional enforcement

result.methodology_note: Raw rate (18.4%) = lessons with any cross-domain L-NNN in section body (weak mention). L-964's 0.1% = substantive cross-domain discussion (manual measurement, harder threshold). 184x gap between mention and substantive integration is itself a finding.

mechanism_implemented.what: check_cross_domain_body_text() function added — flags lessons with cross-domain Cites: but no body-text mention of the cited L-NNN

delta.vs_l964_baseline: L-964's 0.1% was substantive discussion; our 18.4% raw / 32.4% cites-based measures mention (weaker). Establishes two-tier metric: mention (auto-checkable) vs substantive (manual).
delta.prescription_gap: 67.6% of cross-domain Cites: headers lack even weak body mention. Structural flag now active.

verdict: PARTIAL — baseline established, structural mechanism implemented. Two-tier metric clarified. Adoption measurement target: S449 (20 sessions). Success if cites-based rate increases >5pp without additional enforcement.
